We prioritize people, purpose, and performance in all that we do. When the company thrives, <em>you</em> benefit directly. When everyone is invested - literally - it builds a community of accountability, pride and mutual support.<br><br>We look for team members who are <strong>gritty</strong>, <strong>accountable</strong>, and <strong>driven - </strong>with a <strong>mindset rooted in initiative and purpose</strong>, you think like an <strong>entrepreneur</strong> and build strong, trusting relationships through clear communication and thoughtful dialogue, even in challenging conversations.<br><br>Additional Information<br><br>Chandos is an equal opportunity employer and we are committed to a diverse, equitable, and inclusive culture and this includes our recruitment and hiring process.<br><br>We recognize that our differences are our greatest strengths. We uncover talent. We nurture talent. And then we give people the tools they need to use their talent to create a positive impact.<br><br><ul><li>We are 100% employee owned. Every person who works for Chandos can become an owner. There is only one class of units at Chandos, because we believe everyone should be treated equal. We talk about being an entrepreneurial-minded organization and you can be sure we mean it!</li><li>Competitive wages</li><li>Well rounded employer paid benefits program including health, dental, vision care and employee assistance program</li><li>RRSP matching program to aid and prepare employees for long-term financial security</li><li>Educational assistance for employees who wish to pursue job-performance enhancing education</li><li>Recognition and annual salary reviews program</li><li>Leadership conferences that allow employees to connect and collaborate</li><li>Regular company-wide engagement opportunities<br><br></li></ul><strong>Vacancy Status<br><br></strong>We are actively recruiting for this position.<br><br><strong>AI Disclosure<br><br></strong>Chandos is committed to transparent recruitment. Our internal team does not use artificial intelligence (AI) to <strong>screen</strong>, <strong>assess</strong>, or <strong>select</strong> applicants (though we do use AI tools for administrative interview transcription). However, some of our external staffing partners may utilize AI to <strong>screen</strong>, <strong>assess</strong>, or <strong>select</strong> candidates during their initial workflows. All final hiring decisions at Chandos are made personally by our hiring team.<br><br><strong>Compensation Transparency<br><br></strong><em>Expected salary band for this role is (depending on experience): </em>$120,000 to $148,000<br><br><em>The base salary range for this role is listed above and may vary based on your experience. <br><br></em><em>This position also includes a<strong> competitive bonus program.</strong> Throughout the interview process, we芒聙聶ll discuss your background to determine the best alignment for your experience level.</em> senior, ops, education Edmonton, 2026-06-12T03:28:55+00:00 1133309 https://remoteOK.com/remote-jobs/remote-confidential-search-chandos-construction-1133309 https://remoteOK.com/assets/logo-square.png Learning &amp; Development Specialist HealthCorps <strong><strong>Who We Are<br><br></strong></strong>We're HealthCorps - a national non-profit organization committed to improving lives by addressing health challenges in communities through programming that includes innovative and engaging experiences for teens in education, leadership, and service learning. At the heart of our model in near-peer mentorship: we partner with local colleges and universities to deploy college-age mentors into schools, where they connect with students in a way that few others can, as relatable role models who inspire teens to become change agents within their families, schools and neighborhoods. Our program is grounded in the understanding that limited access to health education can lead to a lifetime of social, emotional, and physical challenges.<br><br>Our values drive the work we do here at HealthCorps. We are mission-obsessed, have an entrepreneurial mindset, value that our people are our power, know that teamwork makes the dream work, and believe that there is empowerment in influencing change, both within our teams as well as the communities we serve.<br><br><strong>Where You Fit In<br><br></strong>HealthCorps reaches tens of thousands of students every year, learning about nutrition, mental health, and fitness from our college mentors. This new role is the connective tissue between curriculum design and program delivery 芒聙聰 ensuring what we build lands with the mentors who deliver it and the students they serve.<br><br>Our curriculum team builds great health education content, and our college mentors, supported by their Regional Manager, bring it to life with students. As HealthCorps' Learning &amp; Development Specialist, you'll own the mentor training and development experience and collaborate across departments to keep it best-in-class. You'll coach mentors to be confident, skilled facilitators of the curriculum. You'll also build their fluency in the program administration that surrounds it: activity logs, proof of programming, photos and stories, and site communication. You'll partner with our curriculum team to pressure-test activities before they hit the field, so what we ask mentors to deliver is realistic given the time, setting, and training they have. Mentors work across both classroom and club-based settings with middle and high school students, each with their own dynamics and engagement levels, and our training needs to account for all of it.<br><br>This role is also a professional development engine for our mentors, many of whom are pre-health students heading into careers in medicine, public health, and healthcare. The coaching and training you provide will shape not just their time at HealthCorps, but how they show up as future healthcare professionals.<br><br>You'll report to the Manager of Mento
